title: Refuse and salvage occupations
description: Collect data on work environments for analysis by occupational health and safety specialists. Implement and conduct evaluation of programs designed to limit chemical, physical, biological, and ergonomic risks to workers. All grounds maintenance workers not listed separately. Inspect agricultural commodities, processing equipment, and facilities, and fish and logging operations, to ensure compliance with regulations and laws governing health, quality, and safety. (Based on 10 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Inspect trucks prior to beginning routes to ensure safe operating condition, refuel trucks or add other fluids, check road or weather conditions to determine how routes will be affected, and communicate with dispatchers concerning delays, unsafe sites, accidents, equipment breakdowns, or other maintenance problems.
Drive trucks, following established routes, through residential streets or alleys or through business or industrial areas, collect refuse or recyclable materials, and dump them at disposal sites.
Clean trucks or compactor bodies after routes have been completed and fill out defective equipment reports.
Make special pickups of recyclable materials, such as food scraps, used oil, discarded computers, or other electronic items, and organize schedules for refuse collection.

Tag garbage or recycling containers to inform customers of problems, such as excess garbage or inclusion of items that are not permitted, investigate incidents or violations, and issue notices and recommend corrective actions when infractions or problems are found.

Train workers in safety procedures related to green jobs, such as the use of fall protection devices or maintenance of proper ventilation during wind turbine construction and verify availability or monitor use of safety equipment, such as hearing protection or respirators.

Collect data regarding potential hazards from new equipment or products linked to green practices and conduct worker studies to determine whether specific instances of disease or illness are job-related.

Inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications and conduct inspections to ensure compliance with health and safety regulations.
Prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, production, or work-related accidents and maintain logbooks of daily activities, including areas visited or activities performed.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials and resolve worker problems or collaborate with employees to assist in problem resolution.

emerging_tasks.task: Inspect uncrewed aircraft systems, such as drones, to ensure compliance with safety and operation regulations.
